issues.title: What triggers policy updates?
issues.author: mirjak
issues.body:
The config_set event includes "network_change" as a reason for policy updates. However, HEv3 does not explicitly define a
  mechanism where the configurable policy values (delays, preferred family, etc.) change in response to a network event.

HEv3 mentions network changes in the context of:
 - Flushing historical data when the device changes networks (Section 5.3)
 - Enabling IPv6-only/NAT64 features on IPv6-mostly networks (Section 8)

But these are behavioral adaptations within a fixed policy, not policy parameter changes.

Should "network_change" remain as a config_set reason in this qlog schema, or should it be left to the HEv3 spec to define if/when policy changes in response to network events? Do we need other reasons?
